pages.name: Home
pages.description: Documentation homepage introducing the API and linking to all major sections, with hero, quick-start tasks, and global search.
primary_functions: Orient users to the API platform and its capabilities
Provide quick access to API Reference, Guides, Webhooks, SDKs, and Authentication sections
Offer global search across documentation content
Highlight common developer tasks and quick-start paths

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'API Reference' header navigation link or primary call-to-action button
Click a quick-start task card that deep-links to a specific endpoint (e.g., POST /projects or GET /orders)
Click the 'Guides' header navigation link
Click the 'Webhooks' header navigation link or a featured webhooks tile
Click the 'SDKs' header navigation link or a featured SDK tile
Click the 'Authentication' header navigation link or an authentication quick-start

access_methods.description: Primary entry point via the 'Home' header navigation link or root URL
Can be accessed directly by visiting index.html

pages.name: API Reference
pages.description: Single-page API reference with left-hand resource sidebar, endpoint detail panels, version selector, Try-It console, and multi-language code samples.
primary_functions: Browse API resources and endpoints via a left-hand sidebar (e.g., Projects, Users, Orders, Payments)
View detailed endpoint documentation including HTTP method, path, parameters, request/response schemas, and examples
Interact with a Try-It / API Explorer console for supported endpoints such as GET /users and GET /orders
Switch API versions for applicable endpoints (e.g., compare v1 and v2 behavior for GET /orders/{id})
View multi-language code samples (including Node.js and cURL) and edit inline payload examples
Copy fully configured code samples, including modified Node.js examples for POST /projects

incoming_params.description: API resource slug (e.g., 'projects', 'users', 'orders', 'payments') to pre-select a resource in the sidebar.
Internal endpoint identifier used to scroll to the corresponding endpoint details panel.
API version to select in the version selector (e.g., 'v1', 'v2', or 'unversioned').
When set to 'true', automatically opens the Try-It / API Explorer panel for the selected endpoint.
Name of a parameter (e.g., 'created_after', 'per_page', 'status', 'page', 'sort', 'include') to visually highlight in the parameters and Try-It console.
Code sample language key (e.g., 'node_js', 'curl') to select the appropriate tab in the code samples area.
Initial endpoint sub-view to show, such as 'docs', 'code', or 'try'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Authentication' link from an endpoint description or authorization note
Click 'View rate limits for this endpoint' within an endpoint details panel
Click 'View Python SDK example' from an endpoint details panel
Click 'Learn about webhooks' from a webhook-related endpoint description
Click a breadcrumb or logo that returns to the documentation home

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'API Reference' link in the global header navigation.
Deep-linked with resource/endpoint/version query parameters for specific endpoints or console configurations.
Can be opened directly at api_reference.html to show the default resource and endpoint.

pages.name: Guides Overview
pages.description: Overview of conceptual and how-to guides that organize topics like getting started, rate limits, and webhooks.
primary_functions: List and categorize high-level guides (e.g., Getting Started, Rate Limits, Webhooks, Error Handling)
Provide clear navigation links to specific guides such as the Rate Limits page
Help users choose the right guide for their current task or learning goal

incoming_params.description: Optional guide category slug to pre-select a category (e.g., 'limits', 'webhooks').
Optional guide identifier to scroll to a specific guide entry within the overview.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Rate Limits' guide entry
Click the 'Webhooks' or equivalent webhook-related guide entry
Click a guide entry that recommends reading about authentication methods

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Guides' link in the global header navigation.
Can be accessed directly via guides.html.
Specific categories or guides can be highlighted via the category and guide query parameters.

pages.name: Rate Limits
pages.description: Dedicated documentation explaining rate limiting behavior, headers, per-endpoint limits, and client best practices.
primary_functions: Explain global rate limiting concepts, headers, and retry guidance
Present a table of per-endpoint rate limits, including the POST /payments creation endpoint
Allow users to scan or filter the table to locate specific endpoints and see their per-minute limits
Provide best practices for designing clients that respect rate limits and handle 429 responses

incoming_params.description: Endpoint identifier used to focus the rate limits table on a specific endpoint such as POST /payments.
HTTP method (e.g., 'post', 'get') used to filter the rate limits table.
Endpoint path template (e.g., '/payments') used to filter or highlight rows in the rate limits table.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the endpoint name or path within a rate limit rule row
Click a 'View endpoint details' link associated with a specific rate limit rule
Click a breadcrumb link back to the documentation homepage

access_methods.description: Linked from the Guides Overview page and other inline references as rate_limits.html.
Accessible from the 'Rate Limits' link in the global footer navigation.
Supports deep-linking to specific endpoints via endpointId, method, and path query parameters.

pages.name: Webhooks Overview
pages.description: Overview page for webhook concepts, configuration, delivery mechanics, retries, signatures, and event group navigation.
primary_functions: Explain webhook concepts, delivery mechanics, retries, and signature verification
Describe how to configure webhook endpoints in the dashboard or via API
List webhook event groups (e.g., Invoice events) with links to detailed event group pages like Invoice Events
Guide users toward examples and troubleshooting tips for webhook integrations

incoming_params.description: Optional webhook event group slug (e.g., 'invoice_events') used to highlight a specific group in the event groups list.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Invoice events' group card or list item
Click a link that describes managing webhook endpoints via API
Click a breadcrumb back to the documentation home

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Webhooks' link in the global header navigation.
Can be opened directly at webhooks.html as the main webhooks entry point.
Supports highlighting a specific webhook event group via the group query parameter.

pages.name: Webhooks  Invoice Events
pages.description: Invoice-focused webhook documentation showing invoice-related events and their example payloads, including invoice.paid.
primary_functions: List invoice-related webhook events such as invoice.created, invoice.sent, and invoice.paid
Provide expandable or navigable detail sections for each event, including invoice.paid
Show example deliveries and raw JSON payloads for invoice.paid and other invoice events
Allow users to copy complete example JSON payloads for events like invoice.paid using a copy control

incoming_params.description: Webhook event group slug. When set to 'invoice_events', loads the Invoice events group in the sidebar or header.
Specific webhook event key (e.g., 'invoice.paid') whose details and example payload should be scrolled into view and expanded.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a breadcrumb link back to the Webhooks overview
Click a 'Related REST API' link in an invoice webhook event description

access_methods.description: Linked directly from the Webhooks Overview event groups list for invoice-related events.
Supports deep-linking to specific events such as invoice.paid via the event query parameter.

pages.name: SDKs Overview
pages.description: Overview page listing supported client libraries with links to language-specific SDK documentation such as the Python SDK.
primary_functions: Present a list or grid of available SDKs (e.g., Python, Node.js, Java) with short descriptions
Link to language-specific SDK documentation pages such as the Python SDK page
Explain at a high level why and when to use an SDK versus direct HTTP calls

incoming_params.description: Optional language key (e.g., 'python', 'node_js') to pre-highlight a specific SDK card.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Python SDK' card or link
Click a link comparing SDK usage to raw API requests

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'SDKs' link in the global header navigation.
Can be accessed directly via sdks.html.
Supports highlighting a particular SDK via the language query parameter.

pages.name: Python SDK
pages.description: Language-specific documentation for the Python SDK, including installation, authentication, and modules such as Customers.
primary_functions: Explain how to install, configure, and authenticate the Python SDK
Provide a sidebar or contents list of modules/resources (e.g., Customers, Orders, Payments)
List available methods in the Customers section, including delete_customer or equivalent delete actions
Show a detailed method page or expandable section with example code for deleting a customer by ID
Allow users to copy Python example code blocks, including the delete call with the customer_id argument

incoming_params.description: Identifier for the SDK being viewed (e.g., 'python-sdk') so that this page can load the correct SDK metadata.
Module slug to select in the SDK sidebar (e.g., 'customers', 'orders').
SDK method name to focus on (e.g., 'delete_customer') and scroll into view.
Optional code example identifier (e.g., 'delete_by_id') used to highlight a specific example snippet.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'View underlying REST API endpoint' link in a method details panel
Click 'Learn more about authentication' from the SDK authentication section

access_methods.description: Linked directly from the SDKs Overview page when the Python SDK card is selected.
Supports deep-linking to specific modules and methods via sdkId, module, and method query parameters.

pages.name: Authentication
pages.description: Authentication reference describing API key and bearer token usage with multi-language examples, including cURL commands.
primary_functions: Explain supported authentication schemes (e.g., API key, bearer token) and where to use them
Provide a dedicated API Key / Bearer Token subsection with code samples in multiple languages
Show a cURL example that includes an Authorization: Bearer <api_key> header
Allow users to select the cURL tab and copy the full authenticated cURL command

incoming_params.description: Authentication method identifier (e.g., 'api_key', 'bearer_token') used to select the relevant section on load.
Code example language key (e.g., 'curl') used to pre-select the language tab for authentication examples.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a link suggesting to 'Return to API Reference' after reading auth details
Click a link recommending use of an SDK for managing authentication

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Authentication' link in the global header navigation.
Can be visited directly via authentication.html.
Supports deep-linking to specific methods and languages via the method and lang query parameters.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the API platform, its mission, use cases, stability guarantees, and roadmap.
primary_functions: Describe the APIs mission, core use cases, and target audience
Summarize stability guarantees, versioning policy, and long-term roadmap at a high level

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a link to view the detailed changelog for historical context
Click a breadcrumb or 'Back to docs' link

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'About' link in the global footer navigation.
Can be opened directly at about.html.

pages.name: Support
pages.description: Support hub for developers, listing available help channels and guidance on reporting issues or requesting features.
primary_functions: List available support channels such as email, forums, or issue trackers
Provide guidance on what information to include when reporting problems
Link to status pages or other operational resources if applicable

incoming_params.description: Optional support channel identifier to focus on a preferred contact method.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a link suggesting to review FAQs before contacting support
Click an external 'Status Page' link (note: external URL, not part of this architecture)

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Support' link in the global footer navigation.
Can be accessed directly via support.html.
Optional channel query parameter can pre-select a recommended support channel.

pages.name: Changelog
pages.description: Chronological log of API, SDK, and documentation changes with tags for endpoints and breaking changes.
primary_functions: List releases and changes with dates, including new endpoints and behavior changes
Highlight breaking changes, deprecations, and migration notes
Allow users to scan historical changes relevant to their integration

incoming_params.description: Optional tag filter (e.g., 'orders', 'webhooks') used to limit changelog entries.
Endpoint identifier used to filter changelog entries related to a specific API endpoint.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an endpoint link inside a changelog entry
Click a link to learn more about the API's long-term roadmap

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Changelog' link in the global footer navigation.
Can be visited directly via changelog.html.
Supports filtering entries via tag and endpointId query parameters.

pages.name: FAQ
pages.description: Frequently asked questions organized by topic, with links into deeper reference and guide pages.
primary_functions: Answer common questions about authentication, rate limits, webhooks, and SDK usage
Provide quick troubleshooting tips for typical integration problems
Link to deeper reference or guide pages for more detailed explanations

incoming_params.description: FAQ category slug (e.g., 'authentication', 'rate_limits', 'webhooks', 'sdks', 'troubleshooting') used to pre-filter FAQ entries.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Read more about API key authentication' link in an authentication-related FAQ entry
Click a 'See full rate limits table' link in a rate-limiting FAQ entry
Click a 'Learn more about webhooks' link in a webhook-related FAQ entry
Click 'View SDK docs' from an SDK-related FAQ entry
Click 'Jump to Users API' from a FAQ answer discussing user-related endpoints

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'FAQ' link in the global footer navigation.
Can be accessed directly at faq.html.
Supports focusing on a specific FAQ category via the category query parameter.

header_links.description: Endpoint reference with Try-It console and code samples
Conceptual guides, including links to Rate Limits and other topics
Overview of webhook configuration and event categories
Overview of available client libraries, including Python
Authentication methods and examples, including cURL

footer_links.description: Direct link to the full API reference
Details on global and per-endpoint rate limits
History of API and documentation changes
How to get help or report issues
Frequently asked questions and quick answers
